question_text: A 3 kg object is lifted 5 m vertically and then pushed horizontally across a rough surface for 8 m before stopping. Which of the following best describes the energy transformations that occur?

options.label: A
options.text: Chemical energy → gravitational potential energy → kinetic energy → thermal energy

options.label: B
options.text: Gravitational potential energy → kinetic energy → gravitational potential energy

options.label: C
options.text: Kinetic energy → gravitational potential energy → thermal energy

options.label: D
options.text: Thermal energy → kinetic energy → gravitational potential energy

explanation: The student uses chemical energy from their muscles to lift the object, which becomes gravitational potential energy. When pushing horizontally, this is converted to kinetic energy. Friction on the rough surface converts kinetic energy into thermal energy (heat), which is why the object stops.
